explanation: The Sigma rule detects the execution of splwow64.exe without any command line parameters. This command represents the exact scenario described by the rule.
Inserting trailing spaces in the command line is a safe mutation technique that does not alter the behavior of the process but may bypass simplistic detection based on exact string matching.
Enclosing the executable name in quotes is a safe mutation technique that does not change the execution behavior but may evade detection based on unquoted string patterns.
Adding an unmatched quote at the end is a safe mutation technique that may bypass detection if the rule does not account for such cases.
